primary_subject: Ruins of Mohenjo-daro, Indus Valley Civilization

image_requests.prompt: Wide cinematic establishing shot of ancient Mohenjo-daro archaeological ruins, crumbling brown fired-brick walls and multi-level platforms stretching across a dry dusty plain, warm amber golden-hour light casting long low shadows, hazy atmospheric depth, intricate brick grid patterns visible on ground level, empty and completely silent, no people, muted earthy warm ochre tones, historical documentary photography style, slight film grain, shallow depth of field on horizon
image_requests.purpose: Full-frame hero background — the crumbling Indus Valley ruins fill every pixel, letting the image answer the question posed by JUST VANISHED before the viewer finishes reading it

html: JUSTVANISHED
why: The entire frame is surrendered to the ruins because the image does the narrative work before a single word registers — crumbling brick at scale communicates 'ancient, dead civilization' in 0.05 seconds. The text is split intentionally: JUST sits small and intimate above, VANISHED towers at 224px, architectural and massive, its physical scale rhyming with the ruined city beneath it. A sepia-amber color grade and deep bottom gradient make the treatment feel like a faded archaeological photograph rather than a horror graphic, which keeps the sleep-channel's meditative tone intact while JUST VANISHED delivers the eerie dread. At phone thumbnail size, VANISHED in condensed Oswald bold is the only word that needs to land — the ruins answer what it refers to the instant the eye lands on the image.
title: Boring History for Sleep: The Mystery of the Indus Valley Civilization
